info.title: Coyote API
info.description: Welcome to the Coyote API documentation!

get.description: Get information about the current cluster

post.description:
Initialize this node as the leader of a new cluster

This operation may only be performed against a node which has not been
initialized and is not currently a member of a cluster.

Remove a node from the cluster.

This operation executes immediately and the node must be wiped and reset
before it can safely be added to the cluster.
Complete an idempotent request with a response
Abandon an idempotent request (remove lock without saving response)
Creates or updates a msgs namespace with the given name.
Gets a msgs namespace by name.
Publishes messages to a topic within a namespace.

Receives messages from a topic using a consumer group.

Each consumer in the group reads from all partitions. Messages are locked by leases for the
specified duration to prevent duplicate delivery within the same consumer group.

Commits an offset for a consumer group on a specific partition.

The topic must be a partition-level topic (e.g. `ns:my-topic~3`). The offset is the last
successfully processed offset; future receives will start after it.

Repositions a consumer group's read cursor on a topic.

Provide exactly one of `offset` or `position`. When using `offset`, the topic must include a
partition suffix (e.g. `ns:my-topic~0`). The `position` field accepts `"earliest"` or
`"latest"` and may be used with or without a partition suffix.

Receives messages from a topic as competing consumers.

Messages are individually leased for the specified duration. Multiple consumers can receive
different messages from the same topic concurrently. Leased messages are skipped until they
are acked or their lease expires.

Acknowledges messages by their opaque msg_ids.

Acked messages are permanently removed from the queue and will never be re-delivered.

Configures retry and DLQ behavior for a consumer group on a topic.

`retry_schedule` is a list of delays (in millis) between retries after a nack. Once exhausted,
the message is moved to the DLQ (or forwarded to `dlq_topic` if set).

Rejects messages, sending them to the dead-letter queue.

Nacked messages will not be re-delivered by `queue/receive`. Use `queue/redrive-dlq` to
move them back to the queue for reprocessing.
Moves all dead-letter queue messages back to the main queue for reprocessing.

Configures the number of partitions for a topic.

Partition count can only be increased, never decreased. The default for a new topic is 1.

get.description: Verify the server is up and running.

HTTPBearer.description: HTTP Bearer token passed in the `Authorization` header

expiry_ms.description: Milliseconds from now until the token expires.

enabled.description: Whether the token is enabled. Defaults to `true`.

expiry_ms.description: Milliseconds from now until the token expires. `None` means expire immediately.

enabled.description: Whether this token is currently enabled.

expiry_ms.description: Milliseconds from now until the old token expires. `None` means expire immediately.

cluster_id.description:
The unique ID of this cluster.pub(crate)

This value is populated on cluster initialization and will never change.

cluster_name.description:
The name of this cluster (as defined in the config)

This value is not replicated and should only be used for debugging.

this_node_id.description: The unique ID of the node servicing this request

this_node_state.description: The cluster state of the node servicing this request

this_node_last_committed_timestamp.description: The timestamp of the last transaction committed on this node

nodes.description: A list of all nodes known to be in the cluster

Consistency.description:
Consistency level for reads.

Strong consistency (also known as linearizability) guarantees that a read will see all previous
writes. Weak consistency allows stale reads, but can save one or more round trip to the leader.

ttl.description: TTL in seconds for the cached response
TTL in seconds for the lock/response

oneOf.description: Request was already completed, cached response returned

success.description: Whether the operation succeeded or was a noop due to pre-conditions.

version.description:
Opaque version token for optimistic concurrency control.
Pass as `version` in a subsequent `set` to perform a conditional write.

If set, the write only succeeds when the stored version matches this value.
Use the `version` field from a prior `get` response.

success.description: Whether the operation succeeded or was a noop due to pre-conditions.

key.description: Optional partition key. Messages with the same key are routed to the same partition.

node_id.description:
A unique ID representing this node.

This will never change unless the node is erased and reset

address.description: The advertised inter-server (cluster) address of this node.

state.description: The last known state of this node

last_committed_log_index.description: The index of the last log applied on this node

last_committed_term.description: The raft term of the last committed leadership

retry_after_ms.description: Milliseconds until enough tokens are available (only present when allowed is false)
Milliseconds until at least one token is available (only present when remaining is 0)

refill_amount.description: Number of tokens to add per refill interval

refill_interval_ms.description: Interval in milliseconds between refills (minimum 1 millisecond)

ValidationErrorItem.description:
Validation errors have their own schema to provide context for invalid requests eg. mismatched
types and out of bounds values. There may be any number of these per 422 UNPROCESSABLE ENTITY
error.

loc.description:
The location as a [`Vec`] of [`String`]s -- often in the form `["body", "field_name"]`,
`["query", "field_name"]`, etc. They may, however, be arbitrarily deep.

msg.description: The message accompanying the validation error item.

type.description:
The type of error.

Often "type_error" or "value_error", but sometimes with more context like
"value_error.number.not_ge".
